# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-05-21

Gemini 3.5 Flash was released GA at Google I/O 2026—skipping the preview label—and is priced higher than Gemini 2.0 Flash despite being in the 'Flash' (budget) tier. Google has deployed it across most of their key consumer products.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. Gemini 3.5 Flash: more expensive, but Google plan to use it for everything
   If you built cost assumptions on Gemini 2.0 Flash pricing, 3.5 Flash is not a free upgrade—review the pricing page before switching.

2. My domain got abused on Github Pages
   Real subdomain takeover vulnerability class that affects anyone with custom domains on GitHub Pages—even if the original Pages site was deleted or the...

3. GitHub Source Code Breach - TeamPCP Claims Access to Internal Source Code
   The breach could affect the security of GitHub Actions infrastructure, internal tooling, or token handling. GitHub users should watch for an official...

4. Never host your app on Vercel or Railway
   Potential secret leakage scenario. The claim is unconfirmed by Vercel but describes a concrete incident with email evidence cited.

5. How we used Quint to find over 10 bugs in SQLite while hardening Turso
   If you build on SQLite or libsql, some of these bugs may affect you—and Quint is apparently accessible enough for a small team to use effectively.

Full brief with all 40 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-05-21
